immediate_actions: Call bank helpline immediately to block further transactions
Block the linked debit/credit card or UPI ID via your banking app
Report the unauthorized transaction to your bank within 24 hours to limit liability
evidence_required: Transaction ID and exact timestamp
Screenshot of bank SMS or notification
Phone number or UPI ID of the scammer (if known)
reporting_steps: File a formal complaint on the NCRP portal (cybercrime.gov.in)
Contact your nearest cyber police station with printed evidence
Submit a dispute form to your bank branch
prevention_tips: Never share OTPs, PINs, or passwords with anyone
Enable real-time transaction alerts for all accounts
Do not scan QR codes to receive money

immediate_actions: Check your registered email for any 'New Login' or 'Password Changed' alerts
Use the 'Forgot Password' or account recovery feature on the Instagram app
Immediately secure your linked email account by changing its password
evidence_required: Screenshot of suspicious login location/device
Link to your original profile URL
Records of the original registered email ID and phone number
reporting_steps: Report the hack via the Instagram Help Center (help.instagram.com)
Ask friends to report the account as 'Hacked' from their profiles
File a complaint on the national cyber crime portal if financial loss or defamation occurred
prevention_tips: Enable Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) using an authenticator app
Use a strong, unique password for every social media account
Do not click on suspicious 'verified badge' or 'copyright infringement' links sent via DM

immediate_actions: Do not reply or engage with the bully/attacker
Block and mute the abusive accounts immediately
Document everything before reporting in case the attacker deletes the evidence
evidence_required: Screenshots of the abusive messages or comments (do not alter them)
Direct links (URLs) to the abusive posts
Profile URLs and usernames of the attackers
reporting_steps: Report the individual tweets and the abusive accounts directly to the platform
File a complaint on the national cyber crime portal under 'Cyber Bullying/Harassment'
Contact a legal professional if the abuse contains direct physical threats
prevention_tips: Consider making your profile private temporarily
Use platform features to restrict who can reply to your posts
Filter specific words or phrases in your account settings

immediate_actions: Call your bank's customer service to freeze the credit card instantly
Review your recent statements for any other unauthorized transactions
Change your net banking passwords immediately
evidence_required: Credit card statement highlighting the fraudulent charge
SMS or email alerts of unauthorized transactions
Details of the merchant name where the fraud occurred (if visible)
reporting_steps: Dispute the fraudulent charge with your bank immediately (often a zero-liability policy applies if reported promptly)
File a police report if required by the bank to process the dispute
Report to the cyber crime cell for investigation
prevention_tips: Do not save card details on untrusted or unknown websites
Disable international transaction capabilities when not traveling
Set up transaction limits for online and physical purchases

immediate_actions: Disconnect the infected device from the internet (Wi-Fi and Ethernet) immediately to prevent spreading
Do NOT pay the ransom, as there is no guarantee you will get your data back
Boot the system into Safe Mode to run diagnostic scans
evidence_required: Screenshot or picture (taken with another device) of the ransom note
Samples of the encrypted files (do not delete them yet)
The suspicious email, attachment, or link that likely caused the infection
reporting_steps: Report the incident to your national cyber emergency response team (e.g., CERT-In, CISA)
Seek professional incident response or IT support help
Check NoMoreRansom.org to see if a decryption key is publicly available for your specific ransomware
prevention_tips: Maintain regular, offline backups of your critical data (e.g., disconnected external hard drives)
Keep your operating system and antivirus software up to date
Never open suspicious email attachments or click unverified links

immediate_actions: Do not click on any links or download any attachments in the suspicious email
Do not reply to the sender or provide any personal information
If you already clicked a link and entered credentials, immediately change your password for that service
evidence_required: The original phishing email (do not delete it)
If possible, extract the email headers to identify the true sender
Screenshots of the fake login page (if you clicked the link)
reporting_steps: Mark the email as 'Phishing' or 'Spam' using your email provider's built-in reporting tool
Forward the phishing attempt to the anti-phishing working group or the impersonated organization's fraud department
Report to the national cyber crime portal if you lost data or money
prevention_tips: Always verify the sender's actual email address, not just the display name
Hover over links to see the actual destination URL before clicking
Enable 2FA on all important accounts

immediate_actions: Stop all communications and do not transfer any more money to the scammers
Do not pay 'withdrawal fees' or 'taxes' that they demand to release your supposed profits
Secure any legitimate cryptocurrency wallets by transferring funds to a new, secure wallet if possible
evidence_required: Screenshots of chat histories (Telegram, WhatsApp) where the investment was pitched
Transaction IDs (TxID) on the blockchain for any cryptocurrency sent
URLs of the fraudulent trading websites or apps
reporting_steps: File a complaint with the national cyber crime reporting portal with all crypto transaction details
Report the fraudulent exchange or wallet addresses to blockchain analysis firms or the platform where the scam originated from
Contact your local law enforcement agency specializing in financial crimes
prevention_tips: Be extremely wary of 'guaranteed' high returns with zero risk
Do not trust investment advice from random individuals contacting you on social media or messaging apps
Verify the credentials and regulatory licenses of any trading platform before investing

immediate_actions: Post on your real account to warn friends/family not to accept friend requests or send money to the fake account
Do not confront the fake account directly
Ask friends to mass-report the fake profile for impersonation
evidence_required: Link (URL) to the fake profile
Screenshots of the fake profile's page showing your photos and details
Screenshots of any malicious messages sent by the fake account to your contacts
reporting_steps: Use the platform's specific reporting tool for impersonation (e.g., 'Report -> Report Account -> It's pretending to be someone I know')
If the account is scamming people for money or posting defamatory content, report it to the cyber crime police
If it's a serious threat or involves identity theft offline, contact local authorities
prevention_tips: Regularly review your privacy settings and restrict profile visibility to 'Friends Only'
Watermark your personal photos if you are a public figure or are concerned about theft
Regularly search for your own name/images to catch fake profiles early

immediate_actions: Immediately log out all linked devices via WhatsApp Settings > Linked Devices
Re-register your WhatsApp by requesting a new OTP on your phone number
Enable Two-Step Verification in WhatsApp Settings > Account > Two-Step Verification
Warn all your contacts not to send money or respond to any messages from your account
evidence_required: Screenshots of suspicious messages or calls requesting OTP
The phone number that called you asking for OTP
Date and time of the OTP request
reporting_steps: Report the incident directly to WhatsApp by emailing support@whatsapp.com
File a complaint on cybercrime.gov.in under 'Online and Social Media Crimes'
Lodge an FIR at the nearest police station or cyber crime cell
prevention_tips: Never share WhatsApp OTP with anyone, even if they claim to be WhatsApp support
Enable Two-Step Verification with a PIN to add an extra layer of security
Be suspicious of calls from unknown numbers asking for verification codes

immediate_actions: Lock your Aadhaar biometric immediately at myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in or call 1947
File a complaint with UIDAI if Aadhaar is misused
Inform all your banks about the potential identity theft and request account monitoring
Place a fraud alert with CIBIL or credit bureaus to prevent fake loan applications
evidence_required: Evidence of misuse (fake loan documents, unauthorized account openings, etc.)
Your original ID documents for verification
Police complaint copy for credit bureau alerts
reporting_steps: File a complaint at cybercrime.gov.in under 'Other Cyber Crime'
Report to UIDAI if Aadhaar is misused (helpline: 1947)
Lodge an FIR at the local police station — this is mandatory to notify banks and credit bureaus
Notify the Income Tax department if PAN card has been misused
prevention_tips: Never share photocopies of Aadhaar/PAN without a clear reason and destination
Regularly check your CIBIL score for unexpected loans or credit inquiries
Use Virtual ID (VID) instead of your actual Aadhaar number wherever possible

immediate_actions: Stop all further payments to the seller
Take screenshots of the product listing, order confirmation, and all chat messages
File a dispute with your payment provider (UPI, credit card, Paytm) for a refund
evidence_required: Order confirmation emails or messages
Screenshots of the product listing and seller's contact details
Payment receipts and transaction IDs
Communication history with the seller
reporting_steps: Report the fraudulent seller to the e-commerce platform (Amazon, Flipkart) or social media platform
File a consumer complaint at consumerhelpline.gov.in
File a cybercrime complaint at cybercrime.gov.in if you transferred money
Contact your bank to dispute the transaction
prevention_tips: Only purchase from verified sellers with positive reviews and return policies
Use buyer protection payment methods (credit cards) for large purchases
Be suspicious of deals that are significantly below market price
Verify the seller's physical address before paying

immediate_actions: Do NOT pay the extortionist under any circumstances — it will encourage more demands
Stop all communication with the extortionist immediately
Do not delete the evidence — preserve all screenshots, message logs, and contact details
Report and block the account on the platform immediately after saving evidence
evidence_required: Screenshots of all threatening messages
The extortionist's social media profile URLs or phone numbers
Proof of any payments already made (if applicable)
reporting_steps: File an urgent complaint at cybercrime.gov.in or call the helpline 1930
Lodge an FIR at the nearest police station — share all digital evidence
Report the account on the respective platform (Facebook, Instagram, Telegram) for blackmail/extortion
Seek support from a mental health professional if needed
prevention_tips: Be cautious about sharing intimate images or videos online, even with trusted contacts
Review your privacy settings on all social media platforms regularly
Use strong, unique passwords and enable 2FA to prevent account compromise

immediate_actions: Go to facebook.com/hacked and use the self-recovery tool
If locked out, use 'Find your account' using your email, phone, or friend's help
Immediately revoke access to any unknown third-party apps under Settings > Apps and Websites
Alert your friends and family not to respond to any unusual messages from your account
evidence_required: Your Facebook profile URL
Email associated with your Facebook account
Screenshots of suspicious activity or messages sent from your account
reporting_steps: Submit an appeal via the Facebook Help Center (facebook.com/help)
Use the Facebook 'Hacked Account' recovery form
If financial fraud occurred, file a complaint on cybercrime.gov.in
Report the hacked account's activity to Facebook Trust and Safety team
prevention_tips: Enable Facebook's Two-Factor Authentication under Security and Login settings
Review active sessions and log out from all unrecognized locations
Do not click on suspicious links sent via Facebook Messenger
Use a unique, strong password for Facebook that you do not use elsewhere

subcategory: Fake Job Offer / Work From Home Fraud
immediate_actions: Stop all payments to the fake recruiter or company immediately
Do not share your bank account, Aadhaar, or PAN details with unverified recruiters
Block the fraudster and do not delete any evidence
evidence_required: Job offer letter or email from the fraudulent company
All payment receipts sent to the scammer
Screenshots of the job listing and recruiter's contact details
Chat history with the recruiter
reporting_steps: File a complaint at cybercrime.gov.in under 'Financial Fraud'
Report the fraudulent job listing on LinkedIn or the job portal
Lodge an FIR at your local police station
Warn other job seekers by reporting the company on job review sites
prevention_tips: Never pay for a job — legitimate employers do not ask for money
Verify the company's existence on MCA21 (Ministry of Corporate Affairs portal)
Use official company websites and verified email domains for communication
Be cautious of jobs offering very high salaries for minimal qualification

immediate_actions: Do not respond to the stalker's messages or calls
Block the stalker on all platforms immediately
Enable location privacy on all social media accounts and disable location sharing
Inform a trusted person about the situation and keep records of all incidents
evidence_required: Screenshots of all harassing messages, comments, or emails
Dates and times of incidents
The stalker's social media profile and contact details
Any physical evidence if stalking occurs offline too
reporting_steps: File a complaint at cybercrime.gov.in under 'Cyber Stalking/Bullying Against Women/Children'
Lodge an FIR at the local Women's Police Station or Cyber Crime Cell
Contact the National Commission for Women if online harassment is gender-based
Report all abusive accounts to respective social media platforms
prevention_tips: Audit your social media privacy settings and restrict who can see your location, posts, and friend list
Use a unique username that doesn't reveal your real identity on public platforms
Enable 'Two-Factor Authentication' to prevent your accounts from being accessible to the stalker

immediate_actions: Stop all financial transfers to the person immediately
Cease communication and report the profile on the dating app/social media platform
Do NOT send any more personal documents, photos, or money
Run a reverse image search on their profile photo to verify their identity
evidence_required: Screenshots of all conversations
Profile links, photos, and contact details of the scammer
All financial transaction records (bank transfers, gift cards, crypto sent)
Any fake identification documents they may have shared
reporting_steps: Report the profile to the dating app or social media platform for fraud
File a complaint at cybercrime.gov.in under 'Other Cyber Crime'
Report to your bank to dispute any recent transactions
Lodge an FIR with local police, bringing all digital evidence
prevention_tips: Be extremely cautious about developing emotional relationships with people you meet only online
Never send money to someone you have not met in person, regardless of their story
Always verify the person's identity via video call before trusting them
Use reverse image search to check if profile photos are stolen from the internet
